decisions.title: JWT token expiry is 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as was agreed in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails rate-limited to 3 attempts per hour
decisions.detail: Password reset emails must be rate-limited to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour, as agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.

decisions.title: API gateway to use AWS API Gateway with 100 req/min limit per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway and enforce a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user.

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked by auth API spec publication
decisions.detail: The mobile app team is currently blocked until the authentication API specification is published.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Target publish date for auth API spec: 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: The target publish date for the authentication API specification is 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for auth endpoints before Sprint 3 sign-off
decisions.detail: Automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) are required before Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway goes down?
decisions.detail: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down? This question needs to be answered.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?
decisions.detail: It is an open question whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a password reset occurs.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement JWT token generation and validation with a 24-hour expiry.
Implement rate limiting logic for password reset email requests.
Develop automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: Password reset emails must be rate-limited to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour.
Enforce a 100 requests per minute per user limit via the API Gateway.
Authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05.
Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints are required before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours.
The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behaviour if the AWS API Gateway goes down?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated on password reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a valid JWT token is issued, When 24 hours have passed since its issuance, Then subsequent requests using this token must fail due to token expiry with an appropriate error response.
Given a valid JWT token is issued, When less than 24 hours have passed since its issuance, Then subsequent requests using this token must succeed.

Given a user attempts to request a password reset, When three password reset requests are made for the same user within a 60-minute window, Then all three requests are processed successfully, and a password reset email is sent for each valid attempt.

Given a user has made three password reset requests within a 60-minute window, When a fourth password reset request is made for the same user within the same 60-minute window, Then the request must be rejected with a clear rate-limit error message, and no email is sent.

Given a user has exceeded the password reset rate limit, When more than 60 minutes have passed since the first request that initiated the rate limit window, Then the user can successfully request another password reset email.
Given a user makes API requests, When the user makes 100 requests within a 60-second window, Then all 100 requests are processed successfully.

Given a user has made 100 API requests within a 60-second window, When the user makes the 101st request within the same 60-second window, Then the request must be rejected with an HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) status code.
Given a user has exceeded the API request rate limit, When the 60-second rate limit window resets, Then subsequent requests from the user are processed successfully.
test_scenarios: Verify an authenticated API request succeeds using a JWT token 23 hours and 59 minutes after issuance.
Verify an authenticated API request fails using a JWT token 24 hours and 1 minute after issuance, receiving an expiry error.
Send 3 password reset requests for a user within a 30-minute period and confirm that 3 password reset emails are sent and each request is successful.
Send 4 password reset requests for a user within a 30-minute period and confirm the 4th request is rejected with a rate-limit error.
Send 4 password reset requests for a user, confirming the 4th is rejected. Wait 61 minutes from the first request, then send a 5th request and confirm it succeeds.
Send 100 API requests for a user within a 50-second window and confirm all requests return a successful status code.
Send 101 API requests for a user within a 50-second window and confirm the 101st request returns an HTTP 429 status code.
Trigger the API rate limit for a user (101 requests in 50 seconds), wait 61 seconds from the first request, then send another request and confirm it succeeds.
edge_cases: Test JWT token expiry behavior across daylight saving time changes or system clock adjustments.
Simultaneous password reset requests from multiple devices for the same user account.
Password reset requests for non-existent user accounts – confirm if they consume the rate limit.
Rapid, concurrent API requests from a single user immediately preceding and exceeding the 100 req/min limit.
Testing API rate limits when multiple distinct users simultaneously approach their respective limits.
unresolved_behaviors: What is the expected user experience or system behavior if the API gateway becomes unavailable? (Owned by PM/QA)
Should active refresh tokens be invalidated when a user successfully resets their password? (Owned by PM/QA)

decision_history: JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as agreed in Sprint 2.
Password reset emails must be rate-limited to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour, as agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.
The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway and enforce a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) are required before Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: Mobile app team is blocked by the publication of the authentication API specification.
risks: Unclear fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down (owned by SA). This poses a critical availability risk to the service.
Potential security vulnerability if refresh token invalidation on password reset is not clearly defined or correctly implemented (inferred from D008).
open_questions: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset? (PM/QA owned - requires clarification of security requirements).
milestones: Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints must be completed before Sprint 3 sign-off

system_requirements: The system SHALL ensure JWT tokens expire after 24 hours.
The system SHALL rate-limit password reset email attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour per user.
The system SHALL enforce a request limit of 100 requests per minute per user via the API Gateway.
The system SHALL include automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.
data_flows: User requests SHALL flow through the AWS API Gateway to backend authentication services (inferred from D003).
Authentication service SHALL issue JWT tokens to authenticated users (inferred from D001).
Authentication service SHALL send password reset emails to users (inferred from D002).
The Authentication API specification SHALL be published for consumption by the Mobile App Team (D004, D005).
interface_contracts: The Authentication API specification MUST be formally defined and published. This specification SHALL include the login, refresh token, and password reset endpoints (inferred from D006).
The Authentication API login endpoint SHALL return a JWT token with an expiry of 24 hours (D001, inferred from D006).
The Authentication API password reset endpoint SHALL trigger a password reset email and be subject to a rate limit of 3 attempts per hour per user (D002, inferred from D006).

constraints.text: The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.
The API Gateway MUST enforce a maximum rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway component SHALL be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway experiences an outage or becomes unavailable? (D007)
How should refresh tokens be handled (e.g., invalidated) when a user performs a password reset? (D008)

stakeholder.business_summary: Progress is being made on the authentication system, with key security and performance decisions confirmed. A major roadblock is the mobile app development, which is currently blocked until the authentication API specification is published by July 5th. We also have critical open questions regarding system resilience and user account security that require immediate attention.
key_impact: Improved user account security by limiting password reset attempts and defining secure session lengths.
Enhanced system stability and protection against misuse through defined request limits.
Ongoing delays to the mobile application's progress until the authentication API specification is released.
Increased reliability of authentication services through mandatory automated testing before project milestones.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan if the central system connector (API gateway) experiences an outage, and how will this affect our users?
For security purposes, should current user sessions automatically end when a user resets their password?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. There are no hallucinations, no overstatements, and all inferences are clearly marked. The quality of each view is high, with clear, actionable information for its intended audience, and no contradictions were found. No prompt injection or sensitive content detected.
